project_type: Java libGDX 1.12.1 desktop game (LWJGL3 backend), roguelike dungeon crawler
build_system: Gradle 8.11 with wrapper (./gradlew). Source dirs: srcDirs = ['src']. Packages: model/, controller/, view/
test_framework: No test framework configured - validation via compile only
verification_instructions: Run ./gradlew classes and confirm BUILD SUCCESSFUL. No unit test suite exists.
setup_instructions: Java 17 is set via mise.toml. Run ./gradlew classes to compile.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET network mode. Java 17 available. No Docker needed. No test suite exists - verify by successful compilation.
contribution_requirements: Commit to phase2-decouple-logic branch and push using github Power push_to_remote tool.
key_patterns: Event-driven architecture: all game objects extend GameEventDispatcher, fire GameEvent via builder pattern. CombatEngine is non-blocking state machine with GameState enum transitions. View layer uses Screen interface, PixelRenderer (320x240 FitViewport), AssetLoader for procedural Pixmap sprites. Boss enemies are separate classes extending Enemy. Screens register as GameEventListener on the engine. No external assets - all sprites/textures generated from Pixmaps at runtime.
directory_structure: src/model/ - pure game logic (16 files: GameCharacter, Player, Enemy, bosses, items/, skills/, status/, events/, enemies/). src/controller/ - game flow (6 files: CombatEngine, GameState, WaveManager, ChestSystem, RunModifiers, EventRoomManager). src/view/ - libGDX rendering (22 files: PlagueOfDanjinGame, DesktopLauncher, rendering/, assets/, sprites/, effects/, screens/, ui/).
